title: Nidularium cruentum
intro: Nidularium cruentum, also known as the 'Raven's Head' or 'Blood-red Nidularium', is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae, native to Brazil. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which provide relief from congestion and soothe the respiratory tract. The bioactive constituents of Nidularium cruentum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Nidularium cruentum is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and bronchitis.
taxonomy: Nidularium cruentum is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. This family is a group of monocots commonly known as the pineapple or bromeliad family. The Bromeliaceae family consists of around 50-60 genera and approximately 3,000 species of plants. Nidularium cruentum is a tropical epiphyte that is native to South America, specifically in the countries of Brazil and Uruguay. It belongs to the genus Nidularium, which is a group of small to medium-sized bromeliads.
